---
title: "Coping with depression"
format: html
---

# Coping With Depression in Recovery

::: callout
Depression can be part of recovery, especially as the brain adjusts after alcohol use. Temporary low mood is common, but ongoing or intense symptoms can increase relapse risk.

Recognizing early signs allows you to respond before depression deepens.

**Work through these steps to identify signs of depression and create a coping plan.**
:::

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/tired.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}


## 1. Check Current Symptoms

In the past week, I’ve experienced:

-   [ ] Low energy\
-   [ ] Sleeping too much or too little\
-   [ ] Loss of interest in activities\
-   [ ] Changes in appetite\
-   [ ] Irritability or sadness\
-   [ ] Isolation\
-   [ ] Increased urges to use\
-   [ ] Difficulty completing daily tasks\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

## 2. What are your early signs of depression?

## 3. How does depression affect your behavior?

## 4. How does it affect your recovery?

## 5. Coping Actions

Choose 2–3 strategies you can use:

-   [ ] Move your body\
-   [ ] Talk to someone\
-   [ ] Plan one small activity\
-   [ ] Keep a routine\
-   [ ] Get outside\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. My Action Plan

When I notice these signs, I will:

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

## 7. Support Plan

If symptoms continue or worsen:

-   [ ] I will talk to a counselor or professional\
-   [ ] I will reach out to someone I trust

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Remember: Depression is a signal to respond—not something to ignore or face alone**
